highlights.text: One of those cycles is ES goes elevator down in a vertical flush cutting through every support.
I wrote on Monday at 4pm: “The only possible option here would be a Failed Breakdown of Monday’s daily low which is at 6832 now.

In doing so ES broke out that stubborn 6925 range it had been stuck in all week, but unfortunately, couldn’t stick in on the first shot and yesterday we collapsed back into the range going elevator down into the low 6840s this morning.
From an execution perspecive we would want to wait for a Failed Breakdown, as always.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “The obvious trade here is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at ~6870.” We recovered that, and rallied slowly the rest of the day.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: All Week SPX Went Nowhere. Can We Leave The Range Monday? March 2nd Plan
